docs.title: Welcome to C-ML
docs.text: C-ML is a lightweight and modular machine learning library written in C. It is designed for educational purposes and lightweight applications, providing essential components for building and training neural networks.

docs.title: Why Choose C-ML?
docs.text:
• Lightweight: Minimal dependencies and optimized for performance.
  • Modular: Use only the components you need.
  • Educational: Learn the fundamentals of machine learning by exploring the source code.

docs.title: Get Started
docs.text:
• Installation Guide
  • Features Overview
  • Usage Examples

docs.title: Contribute
docs.text:
We welcome contributions! Check out the Contributing Guidelines to get started.

Tip: Use the navigation bar on the left to explore the documentation.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
Contributions are welcome! Please follow these steps:

  1. Fork the repository.
  2. Create a new branch for your feature or bug fix.
  3. Commit your changes with clear messages.
  4. Submit a pull request.

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
• Follow consistent indentation and naming conventions.
  • Use meaningful variable names.
  • Add comments to explain complex logic.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
• Check for null pointers and invalid inputs.
  • Use fprintf to log errors and exit gracefully.

docs.title: Memory Management
docs.text:
• Allocate memory dynamically where necessary.
  • Free allocated memory to prevent leaks.

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
• Write unit tests for all functions.
  • Use assertions to validate expected behavior.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• 🧩 Layers: Dense, Dropout
  • ⚡ Activations: ReLU, Sigmoid, Tanh, Softmax, ELU, Leaky ReLU, Linear
  • 📉 Loss Functions: Mean Squared Error, Binary Cross-Entropy, Focal Loss, etc.
  • 🚀 Optimizers: SGD, Adam, RMSprop
  • 🔧 Preprocessing: Label Encoding, One-Hot Encoding, Standard Scaler, Min-Max Scaler
  • 🛡️ Regularizers: L1, L2, Combined L1-L2
  • ✅ Test Coverage: Comprehensive unit tests for all modules.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• GCC (GNU Compiler Collection)
  • make build tool
  • Supported Platforms: Linux, macOS, Windows (via WSL)

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository: 

    git clone https://github.com/jaywyawhare/C-ML.git
cd C-ML

  2. Build the project: 

    make

  3. Run the example program: 

    ./bin/main

  4. Run the tests: 

    make test

Note: Ensure that GCC and make are installed and available in your system's PATH.

docs.title: License
docs.text: This project is licensed under the DBaJ-NC-CFL License. See the LICENSE file for details.

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
Run the tests using the make test command: 

make test

Each module has a corresponding test file in the test/ directory. The tests validate the correctness of the implementation and ensure robustness.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
The main.c file demonstrates how to use the library to create a simple neural network. Below is an example:

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <math.h>
#include "../src/Layers/dense.h"
#include "../src/Activations/relu.h"
#include "../src/Loss_Functions/meanSquaredError.h"

int main()
{
    float input[] = {1.0, 2.0, 3.0};
    int input_size = 3;

    float target[] = {0.0, 1.0};
    int output_size = 2;

    DenseLayer dense_layer = {NULL, NULL, 0, 0};
    initializeDense(&dense_layer, input_size, output_size);

    float dense_output[2];
    forwardDense(&dense_layer, input, dense_output);

    for (int i = 0; i < output_size; i++)
    {
        dense_output[i] = relu(dense_output[i]);
    }

    float loss = meanSquaredError(target, dense_output, output_size);
    printf("Loss: %f\n", loss);

    freeDense(&dense_layer);
    return 0;
}

docs.title: ReLU
docs.text:
• Description: Rectified Linear Unit activation function.
  • Function: relu(float x)
  • File: relu.c

docs.title: Sigmoid
docs.text:
• Description: Sigmoid activation function.
  • Function: sigmoid(float x)
  • File: sigmoid.c

docs.title: Tanh
docs.text:
• Description: Hyperbolic tangent activation function.
  • Function: tanH(float x)
  • File: tanh.c

docs.title: Softmax
docs.text:
• Description: Converts logits into probabilities.
  • Function: softmax(float *z, int n)
  • File: softmax.c

docs.title: ELU
docs.text:
• Description: Exponential Linear Unit activation function.
  • Function: elu(float x, float alpha)
  • File: elu.c

docs.title: Leaky ReLU
docs.text:
• Description: Leaky version of ReLU to allow small gradients for negative inputs.
  • Function: leakyRelu(float x)
  • File: leakyRelu.c

docs.title: Linear
docs.text:
• Description: Linear activation function (identity function).
  • Function: linear(float x)
  • File: linear.c

docs.title: Dense Layer
docs.text:
• Description: Fully connected layer where each input is connected to each output.
  • Function: dense(float *input, float *weights, float *bias, int input_size, int output_size)
  • File: dense.c

docs.title: Dropout Layer
docs.text:
• Description: Randomly sets a fraction of input units to zero during training to prevent overfitting.
  • Function: dropout(float *input, float *output, float dropout_rate, int size)
  • File: dropout.c

docs.title: Mean Squared Error
docs.text:
• Description: Measures the average squared difference between predictions and actual values.
  • Function: meanSquaredError(float *y, float *yHat, int n)
  • File: meanSquaredError.c

docs.title: Binary Cross-Entropy
docs.text:
• Description: Loss function for binary classification tasks.
  • Function: binaryCrossEntropyLoss(float *yHat, float *y, int size)
  • File: binaryCrossEntropyLoss.c

docs.title: Focal Loss
docs.text:
• Description: Focuses on hard-to-classify examples by down-weighting easy examples.
  • Function: focalLoss(float *y, float *yHat, int n, float gamma)
  • File: focalLoss.c

docs.title: Mean Absolute Error
docs.text:
• Description: Measures the average absolute difference between predictions and actual values.
  • Function: meanAbsoluteError(float *y, float *yHat, int n)
  • File: meanAbsoluteError.c

docs.title: Mean Absolute Percentage Error
docs.text:
• Description: Measures the percentage difference between predictions and actual values.
  • Function: meanAbsolutePercentageError(float *y, float *yHat, int n)
  • File: meanAbsolutePercentageError.c

docs.title: Root Mean Squared Error
docs.text:
• Description: Square root of the mean squared error.
  • Function: rootMeanSquaredError(float *y, float *yHat, int n)
  • File: rootMeanSquaredError.c

docs.title: Reduce Mean
docs.text:
• Description: Computes the mean of an array of values.
  • Function: reduceMean(float *loss, int size)
  • File: reduceMean.c

docs.title: SGD (Stochastic Gradient Descent)
docs.text:
• Description: Basic optimizer that updates weights using gradients.
  • Function: SGD(float x, float y, float lr, float *w, float *b)
  • File: sgd.c

docs.title: Adam
docs.text:
• Description: Adaptive optimizer combining momentum and RMSprop.
  • Function: Adam(float x, float y, float lr, float *w, float *b, ...)
  • File: Adam.c

docs.title: RMSprop
docs.text:
• Description: Optimizer that scales learning rates based on recent gradients.
  • Function: RMSprop(float x, float y, float lr, float *w, float *b, ...)
  • File: RMSprop.c

docs.title: Standard Scaler
docs.text:
• Description: Scales data to have zero mean and unit variance.
  • Function: standardScaler(float *x, int size)
  • File: standardScaler.c

docs.title: Min-Max Scaler
docs.text:
• Description: Scales data to a specified range (default: [0, 1]).
  • Function: minMaxScaler(float *x, int size)
  • File: minMaxScaler.c

docs.title: Label Encoder
docs.text:
• Description: Encodes categorical labels as integers.
  • Function: labelEncoder(char *x, int size, ...)
  • File: labelEncoder.c

docs.title: One-Hot Encoder
docs.text:
• Description: Encodes categorical labels as one-hot vectors.
  • Function: oneHotEncoding(char *x, int size, ...)
  • File: oneHotEncoder.c

docs.title: L1 Regularization
docs.text:
• Description: Adds the absolute value of weights to the loss function to encourage sparsity.
  • Function: l1(float x, float y, ...)
  • File: l1.c

docs.title: L2 Regularization
docs.text:
• Description: Adds the squared value of weights to the loss function to prevent overfitting.
  • Function: l2(float x, float y, ...)
  • File: l2.c

docs.title: Combined L1-L2 Regularization
docs.text:
• Description: Combines L1 and L2 regularization techniques.
  • Function: l1_l2(float *w, float *dw, float l1, float l2, int n)
  • File: l1_l2.c
